name: What should I look for when choosing a contractor for my bathroom remodel?
headline: What should I look for when choosing a contractor for my bathroom remodel?

mainEntity.name: What should I look for when choosing a contractor for my bathroom remodel?
mainEntity.text: What should I look for when choosing a contractor for my bathroom remodel?

acceptedAnswer.text: For a bathroom remodel in cities like Pacifica, Daly City, and San Bruno, key things to look for in a contractor are **license, insurance, experience, and communication**. In California, the contractor should hold a current state license for the type of work they are doing and carry both liability and workers’ compensation insurance. Ask how many bathroom remodels they’ve done in homes similar to yours and if they have photos and references you can contact. Bathrooms have a lot of plumbing, waterproofing, and tile details, so specific experience matters more than just general construction. Pay attention to how clearly they explain the process, schedule, and payment terms. A detailed written estimate and contract should spell out materials, scope, change-order procedures, cleanup, and who pulls permits. Red flags include: - Vague or handwritten estimates with no detail - Pressure to pay a large cash deposit - No proof of license or insurance - Unwillingness to answer questions or provide references A common pitfall is choosing based only on the lowest price or the fastest promise. The cheapest bid may leave out important items like permits, waterproofing, or quality fixtures. Good contractors are usually clear, organized, and honest about time and cost. Take notes when you meet, compare answers from a few companies, and choose the one that gives you confidence in both their skills and their communication.
